title: Official release package distribution
body:
Hi,
I am sorry if the information is already present somewhere, but I could not find it on my own.

What is the recommended way to install buildah in production? I really like the features of buildah and would like to use it for our build environments, as the scripted build approach offers nice features over our current Dockerfile-based build. Currently we are building on Ubuntu (mostly because of a more current version of Docker) and I was quite surprised what versions of buildah I receive on different operating systems.
From the release notes I was expecting to get v1.7.1 consistently on all platforms, but depending on which platform I was following the instructions from the [installation notes](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md) I get different versions of buildah, Ubuntu does even seem to get unreleased packages.
Could you please advise how I should install packages to obtain the production-ready version of buildah? Thanks for any help in advance.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Get Ubuntu/CentOS/RHEL systems
2. Install buildah from repositories
3. Compare received buildah versions


**Describe the results you received:**
- Ubuntu 18.04 x86_64 -> 1.8-dev (unreleased?)
```
# buildah --version
buildah version 1.8-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.1 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.1 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic

# uname -a
Linux xxx 4.15.0-39-generic #42-Ubuntu SMP Tue Oct 23 15:48:01 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

- Ubuntu 18.04 ppc64le -> 1.8-dev (unreleased?)
```
# buildah --version
buildah version 1.8-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.1 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.1 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic

# uname -a
Linux xxx 4.15.0-38-generic #41-Ubuntu SMP Wed Oct 10 10:57:45 UTC 2018 ppc64le ppc64le ppc64le GNU/Linux
```

- CentOS 7 x86_64 -> 1.5.2 (executed inside a centos:7 docker container)
```
# buildah --version
buildah version 1.5 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

# yum --showduplicates list buildah
Loaded plugins: fastestmirror, ovl
Loading mirror speeds from cached hostfile
 * base: centos.mirrors.nublue.co.uk
 * extras: www.mirrorservice.org
 * updates: www.mirrorservice.org
Installed Packages
buildah.x86_64                                                                                 1.5-2.gite94b4f9.el7.centos                                                                                  @extras
Available Packages
buildah.x86_64                                                                                 1.2-2.gitbe87762.el7                                                                                         extras 
buildah.x86_64                                                                                 1.4-3.gitc8ed967.el7                                                                                         extras 
buildah.x86_64                                                                                 1.5-2.gite94b4f9.el7.centos                                                                                  extras

# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="7 (Core)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="7"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 7 (Core)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:7"
HOME_URL="https://www.centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"

CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-7"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="centos"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7"

# uname -a
Linux 2f370933c7e3 3.10.0-957.10.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Feb 7 07:12:53 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

- RHEL 7 x86_64 -> 1.2 (might be caused by old repositories in our company, not sure about that one)
```
# buildah --version
buildah version 1.2 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server"
VERSION="7.6 (Maipo)"
ID="rhel"
ID_LIKE="fedora"
VARIANT="Server"
VARIANT_ID="server"
VERSION_ID="7.6"
PRETTY_NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server 7.6 (Maipo)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:7.6:GA:server"
HOME_URL="https://www.redhat.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"

REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=7.6
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7.6"

# uname -a
Linux xxx 3.10.0-862.11.6.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Aug 10 16:55:11 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

- RHEL 7 ppc64le -> 1.5 (might be caused by old repositories in our company, not sure about that one, but I was surprised that the versions differ between architectures)
```
# buildah --version
buildah version 1.5 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server"
VERSION="7.6 (Maipo)"
ID="rhel"
ID_LIKE="fedora"
VARIANT="Server"
VARIANT_ID="server"
VERSION_ID="7.6"
PRETTY_NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server 7.6 (Maipo)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:7.6:GA:server"
HOME_URL="https://www.redhat.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"

REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=7.6
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7.6"

# uname -a
Linux xxxx 3.10.0-957.5.1.el7.ppc64le #1 SMP Wed Dec 19 15:44:52 UTC 2018 ppc64le ppc64le ppc64le GNU/Linux
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect to receive the latest released code via the package manager (v1.7.1) on all platforms, at least on RHEL/CentOS/Ubuntu.

comments.body: @lsm5 thoughts or can you fill in the blanks for when/how things are updated on the different distros?
Perhaps the Dockerfile stuff you are working on can solve this issue.
It would also be nice to have binaries published to github release.
let me get back on this once the snap packages are done.
We now have supported container images for this on https://quay.io/buildah

Cool, thanks, looks quite good.
While trying out the container I noticed one minor thing in https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/8e48a65d5b22366c2e4946d6e41788365acc7ae2/contrib/buildahimage/README.md#sample-usage, probably we should adjust the command to run in the loop.
When using the command as defined, the CPU load on a single core goes up to 100% usage: 
```
podman run \
  --detach \
  --name=buildahctr \
  --net=host \
  --security-opt label=disable \
  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
  --device /dev/fuse:rw \
  -v /var/lib/mycontainer:/var/lib/containers:Z \
  quay.io/buildah/stable sh -c 'while true ;do wait; done'
```
![Selection_574](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40577406/59265953-c7407380-8c46-11e9-8e02-83a8484c17a9.png)

When switched from `wait` to `sleep`, that problem disappeared, consumption is shown as 0%:
I used:
```
podman run \
  --detach \
  --name=buildahctr \
  --net=host \
  --security-opt label=disable \
  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
  --device /dev/fuse:rw \
  -v /var/lib/mycontainer:/var/lib/containers:Z \
  quay.io/buildah/stable sh -c 'while true ;do sleep 100000; done'
```
![Selection_575](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40577406/59265962-cd365480-8c46-11e9-81b7-168029a02b47.png)
@konrad-ohms do you have a suggested tweak in mind for the command?

I propose to change the command executed as start command in the container from
```
sh -c 'while true ;do wait; done'
```
to
```
sh -c 'while true ;do sleep 100000; done'
```

That should help resolving the high CPU load.
Thanks @konrad-ohms , I'll give that a whirl.  I've spun up #1665 to track it.

@konrad-ohms are you still looking to install buildah on 18.04? 1.9.2 is available on the PPA. See: https://launchpad.net/~projectatomic/+archive/ubuntu/ppa/+packages

Also, I've set up autobuilds, so new builds should show up within an hour of upstream release.
I don't believe there's further work for this issue.  Please spin up a new one if you've other questions or concerns.
